title: Savory Light Chinese Duck Stir-fry
description: A light and flavorful hangover cure featuring red pepper paste and ovo-vegetarian-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the duck breast and sear until browned on both sides, about 3 minutes per side.
Remove the duck from the skillet and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and reduce heat to medium.
Add the sliced ginger and minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the red pepper paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the soy sauce, hoisin sauce, rice vinegar, and water, and stir to combine.
Add the browned duck back to the skillet and simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the duck is cooked through.
Stir in the cornstarch and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Stir in the beaten egg and cook until the egg is cooked through, about 1-2 minutes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with green onions and scallions before serving.
